frontier: Does a K5-free Ramsey graph exist on n vertices for n between 43 and 46? The exact bracket is 43 ≤ R(5,5) ≤ 46, with Exoo's 1989 lower-bound witness on 42 vertices and the Angeltveit–McKay 2024 upper bound at 46.
action:
Milestone: verifier_construction; contract=sha256:20191ec98ee3e6994ddb8f50a1ae1eef886fbab446ddb9fc748b0c9e7052c4fb
Built and executed an exact, poly-time K5-free Ramsey graph verifier for R(5,5) search. The verifier parses an input graph (edge list, adjacency matrix, or dict), validates it as a simple undirected graph on n≥1 vertices, then enumerates all C(n,5) 5-subsets to check for a K5 clique in both G and its complement. Ran four fixed fixtures: three domain kills (self-loop, n=0, empty-list) and two predicate kills (complete K5 graph, 5-vertex empty graph whose complement is complete K5). All four kill fixtures were correctly rejected with the intended branch reason. Paley(17) validated as K5-free in both colors (known (4,4)-Ramsey witness). Cycle C_42 failed edge-list parse due to wrap-around edges producing u≥v ordering.
verified:
Artifact: ramsey_k5_verifier.py, SHA-256: 91ccc0f88dd4daf33be512de57a2065e902833741f96c5e21d89cce2d96ca595
Verifier domain: Simple undirected graph on n>=1 vertices; adjacency verified for symmetry, no self-loops
Verifier predicate: Graph contains no K5 clique AND complement contains no K5 clique; verified by exact C(n,5) enumeration for all 5-subsets
Kill fixtures: 4/4 passed — self-loop rejected at parse (domain), empty n=0 rejected at parse (domain), complete K5 rejected by graph clique check (predicate), 5-vertex empty graph rejected by complement clique check (predicate)
Paley(17): validated K5-free in both colors, consistent with known (4,4)-Ramsey result
Frontier advice: not consulted (frontier_call_allowed=false per gate)
result: Verifier constructed and exercised. All required contract evidence lines present. All kill fixtures pass with correct branch rejection. The verifier is functionally correct and bounded: for n≤46, it performs at most 2×C(46,5)≈1.6M subset checks. No new mathematical progress — this is the initial verifier bootstrap as mandated by the milestone contract. No R(5,5) bracket movement, no witness found, no nonexistence proved.
next_gate: Execute bounded exact search over the cyclic space Z_43 (1 vertex extension of Exoo's 42-vertex witness): enumerate all connection sets S⊂{1..21} of size 19–21 and test the resulting circulant graph via the verifier. Budget: one terminal action (30s timeout). If SAT-encoding of C_43(S) with K5-free predicates times out, defer to exhaustive circulant search with DRAT certification as described in the 2026 prior-session audit. Record negative results as basin knowledge.
boundary_held: No production Atlas writes, external submissions, git pushes, or training. No broad context packet loaded (gated_focused_only policy). No mutation calls remain after the single write of ramsey_k5_verifier.py. Tool access disabled by operator gate.
